./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:        if index not in SKIP_IN_PATH:
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:            __path_parts = {"index": _quote(index)}
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:            __path = f'/{__path_parts["index"]}/_cache/clear'
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:        index: str,
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:            t.Union[int, t.Union[str, t.Literal["all", "index-setting"]]]
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>Clone an index.</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>Clone an existing index into a new index.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          Each original primary shard is cloned into a new primary shard in the new index.</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>IMPORTANT: Elasticsearch does not apply index templates to the resulting index.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          The API also does not copy index metadata from the original index.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          Index metadata includes aliases, index lifecycle management phase definitions, and cross-cluster replication (CCR) follower information.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          For example, if you clone a CCR follower index, the resulting clone will not be a follower index.</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>The clone API copies most index settings from the source index to the resulting index, with the exception of <code>index.number_of_replicas</code> and <code>index.auto_expand_replicas</code>.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          To set the number of replicas in the resulting index, configure these settings in the clone request.</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <li>First, it creates a new target index with the same definition as the source index.</li>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <li>Then it hard-links segments from the source index into the target index. If the file system does not support hard-linking, all segments are copied into the new index, which is a much more time consuming process.</li>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <li>Finally, it recovers the target index as though it were a closed index which had just been re-opened.</li>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <li>The index must be marked as read-only and have a cluster health status of green.</li>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <li>The target index must not exist.</li>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <li>The source index must have the same number of primary shards as the target index.</li>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <li>The node handling the clone process must have sufficient free disk space to accommodate a second copy of the existing index.</li>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>The current write index on a data stream cannot be cloned.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          In order to clone the current write index, the data stream must first be rolled over so that a new write index is created and then the previous write index can be cloned.</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>NOTE: Mappings cannot be specified in the <code>_clone</code> request. The mappings of the source index will be used for the target index.</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>The <code>_clone</code> API returns as soon as the target index has been added to the cluster state, before any shards have been allocated.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          If, for any reason, the target index can't be allocated, its primary shard will remain unassigned until it can be allocated on that node.</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>Because the clone operation creates a new index to clone the shards to, the wait for active shards setting on index creation applies to the clone index action as well.</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:        :param index: Name of the source index to clone.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:        :param target: Name of the target index to create.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:        :param aliases: Aliases for the resulting index.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:        :param settings: Configuration options for the target index.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:            up to the total number of shards in the index (`number_of_replicas+1`).
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:        if index in SKIP_IN_PATH:
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:            raise ValueError("Empty value passed for parameter 'index'")
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:            "index": _quote(index),
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:        __path = f'/{__path_parts["index"]}/_clone/{__path_parts["target"]}'
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:        index: t.Union[str, t.Sequence[str]],
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:            t.Union[int, t.Union[str, t.Literal["all", "index-setting"]]]
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>Close an index.</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>A closed index is blocked for read or write operations and does not allow all operations that opened indices allow.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          It is not possible to index documents or to search for documents in a closed index.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          Closed indices do not have to maintain internal data structures for indexing or searching documents, which results in a smaller overhead on the cluster.</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>When opening or closing an index, the master node is responsible for restarting the index shards to reflect the new state of the index.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          An error is thrown if the request explicitly refers to a missing index.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:        :param index: Comma-separated list or wildcard expression of index names used
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:        :param allow_no_indices: A setting that does two separate checks on the index
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:            after all expressions are evaluated. If `true`, index expressions that resolve

./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:            up to the total number of shards in the index (`number_of_replicas+1`).
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:        if index in SKIP_IN_PATH:
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:            raise ValueError("Empty value passed for parameter 'index'")

./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:            t.Union[int, t.Union[str, t.Literal["all", "index-setting"]]]
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>Create an index.</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>You can use the create index API to add a new index to an Elasticsearch cluster.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          When creating an index, you can specify the following:</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <li>Settings for the index.</li>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <li>Mappings for fields in the index.</li>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>By default, index creation will only return a response to the client when the primary copies of each shard have been started, or the request times out.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          The index creation response will indicate what happened.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          For example, <code>acknowledged</code> indicates whether the index was successfully created in the cluster, <code>while shards_acknowledged</code> indicates whether the requisite number of shard copies were started for each shard in the index before timing out.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          Note that it is still possible for either <code>acknowledged</code> or <code>shards_acknowledged</code> to be <code>false</code>, but for the index creation to be successful.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          If <code>acknowledged</code> is false, the request timed out before the cluster state was updated with the newly created index, but it probably will be created sometime soon.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          If <code>shards_acknowledged</code> is false, then the request timed out before the requisite number of shards were started (by default just the primaries), even if the cluster state was successfully updated to reflect the newly created index (that is to say, <code>acknowledged</code> is <code>true</code>).</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>You can change the default of only waiting for the primary shards to start through the index setting <code>index.write.wait_for_active_shards</code>.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:        :param index: Name of the index you wish to create. Index names must meet the

./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>For example, a TSDS index that contains metrics sampled every 10 seconds can be downsampled to an hourly index.

./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>A closed index is blocked for read/write operations and does not allow all operations that opened indices allow.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          It is not possible to index documents or to search for documents in a closed index.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          This allows closed indices to not have to maintain internal data structures for indexing or searching documents, resulting in a smaller overhead on the cluster.</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>When opening or closing an index, the master is responsible for restarting the index shards to reflect the new state of the index.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          An error is thrown if the request explicitly refers to a missing index.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>Because opening or closing an index allocates its shards, the <code>wait_for_active_shards</code> setting on index creation applies to the <code>_open</code> and <code>_close</code> index actions as well.</p>

./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>Create or update an index template.</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>Elasticsearch applies templates to new indices based on an wildcard pattern that matches the index name.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          Index templates are applied during data stream or index creation.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          Settings and mappings specified in a create index API request override any settings or mappings specified in an index template.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          Changes to index templates do not affect existing indices, including the existing backing indices of a data stream.</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>You can use C-style <code>/* *\\/</code> block comments in index templates.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>If multiple index templates match the name of a new index or data stream, the template with the highest priority is used.</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>Multiple templates with overlapping index patterns at the same priority are not allowed and an error will be thrown when attempting to create a template matching an existing index template at identical priorities.</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>When multiple component templates are specified in the <code>composed_of</code> field for an index template, they are merged in the order specified, meaning that later component templates override earlier component templates.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          Any mappings, settings, or aliases from the parent index template are merged in next.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          Finally, any configuration on the index request itself is merged.

./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>Reload an index's search analyzers and their resources.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>NOTE: This API does not perform a reload for each shard of an index.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          Instead, it performs a reload for each node containing index shards.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          As a result, the total shard count returned by the API can differ from the number of index shards.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          Because reloading affects every node with an index shard, it is important to update the synonym file on every data node in the cluster--including nodes that don't contain a shard replica--before using this API.

./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>Resolve the specified index expressions to return information about each cluster, including the local &quot;querying&quot; cluster, if included.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          If no index expression is provided, the API will return information about all the remote clusters that are configured on the querying cluster.</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>You use the same index expression with this endpoint as you would for cross-cluster search.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>For each cluster in the index expression, information is returned about:</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <li>Whether the querying (&quot;local&quot;) cluster is currently connected to each remote cluster specified in the index expression. ./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <li>Whether there are any indices, aliases, or data streams on that cluster that match the index expression.</li>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <li>Whether the search is likely to have errors returned when you do the cross-cluster search (including any authorization errors if you do not have permission to query the index).</li>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>For example, <code>GET /_resolve/cluster/my-index-*,cluster*:my-index-*</code> returns information about the local cluster and all remotely configured clusters that start with the alias <code>cluster*</code>.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          Each cluster returns information about whether it has any indices, aliases or data streams that match <code>my-index-*</code>.</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>The ability to query without an index expression was added in version 8.18, so when
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          querying remote clusters older than that, the local cluster will send the index
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          to that index expression even though you didn't request it. If it causes a problem, you can
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          instead include an index expression like <code>*:*</code> to bypass the issue.</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>You may want to exclude a cluster or index from a search when:</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <li>A cluster has no matching indices, aliases or data streams for the index expression (or your user does not have permissions to search them). ./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <li>The index expression (combined with any query parameters you specify) will likely cause an exception to be thrown when you do the search. In these cases, the &quot;error&quot; field in the <code>_resolve/cluster</code> response will be present. (This is also where security/permission errors will be shown.)</li>

./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>Roll over to a new index.</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>TIP: We recommend using the index lifecycle rollover action to automate rollovers. However, Serverless does not support Index Lifecycle Management (ILM), so don't use this approach in the Serverless context.</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>The rollover API creates a new index for a data stream or index alias.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>If you roll over a data stream, the API creates a new write index for the stream.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          The stream's previous write index becomes a regular backing index.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p><strong>Roll over an index alias with a write index</strong></p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>TIP: Prior to Elasticsearch 7.9, you'd typically use an index alias with a write index to manage time series data.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>If an index alias points to multiple indices, one of the indices must be a write index.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          The rollover API creates a new write index for the alias with <code>is_write_index</code> set to <code>true</code>.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          The API also <code>sets is_write_index</code> to <code>false</code> for the previous write index.</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p><strong>Roll over an index alias with one index</strong></p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>If you roll over an index alias that points to only one index, the API creates a new index for the alias and removes the original index from the alias.</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>NOTE: A rollover creates a new index and is subject to the <code>wait_for_active_shards</code> setting.</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p><strong>Increment index names for an alias</strong></p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>When you roll over an index alias, you can specify a name for the new index.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          If you don't specify a name and the current index ends with <code>-</code> and a number, such as <code>my-index-000001</code> or <code>my-index-3</code>, the new index name increments that number.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          For example, if you roll over an alias with a current index of <code>my-index-000001</code>, the rollover creates a new index named <code>my-index-000002</code>.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          This number is always six characters and zero-padded, regardless of the previous index's name.</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>If you use an index alias for time series data, you can use date math in the index name to track the rollover date.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          For example, you can create an alias that points to an index named <code>&lt;my-index-{now/d}-000001&gt;</code>.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          If you create the index on May 6, 2099, the index's name is <code>my-index-2099.05.06-000001</code>.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          If you roll over the alias on May 7, 2099, the new index's name is <code>my-index-2099.05.07-000002</code>.</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:        :param alias: Name of the data stream or index alias to roll over.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:        :param new_index: Name of the index to create. Supports date math. Data streams

./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:        :param dry_run: If `true`, checks whether the current index satisfies the specified

./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>Get index shard stores.</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>The index shard stores API returns the following information:</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <li>Any errors encountered while opening the shard index or from an earlier failure.</li>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:        :param index: List of data streams, indices, and aliases used to limit the request.

./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:            __path = f'/{__path_parts["index"]}/_shard_stores'
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:        index: str,
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:            t.Union[int, t.Union[str, t.Literal["all", "index-setting"]]]
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>Shrink an index.</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>Shrink an index into a new index with fewer primary shards.</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>Before you can shrink an index:</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <li>The index must be read-only.</li>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <li>A copy of every shard in the index must reside on the same node.</li>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <li>The index must have a green health status.</li>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>To make shard allocation easier, we recommend you also remove the index's replica shards.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>The requested number of primary shards in the target index must be a factor of the number of shards in the source index.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          For example an index with 8 primary shards can be shrunk into 4, 2 or 1 primary shards or an index with 15 primary shards can be shrunk into 5, 3 or 1.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          If the number of shards in the index is a prime number it can only be shrunk into a single primary shard
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          Before shrinking, a (primary or replica) copy of every shard in the index must be present on the same node.</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>IMPORTANT: If the source index already has one primary shard, configuring the shrink operation with 'index.number_of_shards: 1' will cause the request to fail. An index with one primary shard cannot be shrunk further.</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>The current write index on a data stream cannot be shrunk. In order to shrink the current write index, the data stream must first be rolled over so that a new write index is created and then the previous write index can be shrunk.</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <li>Creates a new target index with the same definition as the source index, but with a smaller number of primary shards.</li>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <li>Hard-links segments from the source index into the target index. If the file system does not support hard-linking, then all segments are copied into the new index, which is a much more time consuming process. Also if using multiple data paths, shards on different data paths require a full copy of segment files if they are not on the same disk since hardlinks do not work across disks.</li>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <li>Recovers the target index as though it were a closed index which had just been re-opened. Recovers shards to the <code>.routing.allocation.initial_recovery._id</code> index setting.</li>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <li>The target index must not exist.</li>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <li>The source index must have more primary shards than the target index.</li>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <li>The number of primary shards in the target index must be a factor of the number of primary shards in the source index. The source index must have more primary shards than the target index.</li>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <li>The index must not contain more than 2,147,483,519 documents in total across all shards that will be shrunk into a single shard on the target index as this is the maximum number of docs that can fit into a single shard.</li>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <li>The node handling the shrink process must have sufficient free disk space to accommodate a second copy of the existing index.</li>

./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>Split an index into a new index with more primary shards.</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>Before you can split an index:</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>The index must be read-only.</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>You can do make an index read-only with the following request using the add index block API:</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <pre><code>PUT /my_source_index/_block/write
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>The current write index on a data stream cannot be split.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          In order to split the current write index, the data stream must first be rolled over so that a new write index is created and then the previous write index can be split.</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <p>The number of times the index can be split (and the number of shards that each original shard can be split into) is determined by the <code>index.number_of_routing_shards</code> setting.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          For instance, a 5 shard index with <code>number_of_routing_shards</code> set to 30 (5 x 2 x 3) could be split by a factor of 2 or 3.</p>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <li>Creates a new target index with the same definition as the source index, but with a larger number of primary shards.</li>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <li>Hard-links segments from the source index into the target index. If the file system doesn't support hard-linking, all segments are copied into the new index, which is a much more time consuming process.</li>
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/indices.py:          <li>Recovers the target index as though it were a closed index which had just been re-opened.</li>

./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/ccr.py:          <p>A following index takes out retention leases on its leader index.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/ccr.py:          These leases are used to increase the likelihood that the shards of the leader index retain the history of operations that the shards of the following index need to run replication.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/ccr.py:          When a follower index is converted to a regular index by the unfollow API (either by directly calling the API or by index lifecycle management tasks), these leases are removed.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/ccr.py:          However, removal of the leases can fail, for example when the remote cluster containing the leader index is unavailable.
./venv/lib64/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_async/client/ccr.py:          While the leases will eventually expire on their own, their extended existence can cause the leader index to hold more history than necessary and prevent index lifecycle management from performing some operations on the leader index.

./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/synonyms.py:          Even if the analyzer is not used on any field mapping, it still needs to be loaded on the index recovery phase.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/synonyms.py:          <p>If any analyzers cannot be loaded, the index becomes unavailable and the cluster status becomes red or yellow as index shards are not available.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/synonyms.py:          You can migrate an index by creating a new index that does not contain the token filter with the synonyms set, and use the reindex API in order to copy over the index data.

./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>Bulk index or delete documents.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>Perform multiple <code>index</code>, <code>create</code>, <code>delete</code>, and <code>update</code> actions in a single request.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          This reduces overhead and can greatly increase indexing speed.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>If the Elasticsearch security features are enabled, you must have the following index privileges for the target data stream, index, or index alias:</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <li>To use the <code>create</code> action, you must have the <code>create_doc</code>, <code>create</code>, <code>index</code>, or <code>write</code> index privilege. Data streams support only the <code>create</code> action.</li>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <li>To use the <code>index</code> action, you must have the <code>create</code>, <code>index</code>, or <code>write</code> index privilege.</li>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <li>To use the <code>delete</code> action, you must have the <code>delete</code> or <code>write</code> index privilege.</li>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <li>To use the <code>update</code> action, you must have the <code>index</code> or <code>write</code> index privilege.</li>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <li>To automatically create a data stream or index with a bulk API request, you must have the <code>auto_configure</code>, <code>create_index</code>, or <code>manage</code> index privilege.</li>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <li>To make the result of a bulk operation visible to search using the <code>refresh</code> parameter, you must have the <code>maintenance</code> or <code>manage</code> index privilege.</li>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>Automatic data stream creation requires a matching index template with data stream enabled.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>The <code>index</code> and <code>create</code> actions expect a source on the next line and have the same semantics as the <code>op_type</code> parameter in the standard index API.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          An <code>index</code> action adds or replaces a document as necessary.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          To update or delete a document in a data stream, you must target the backing index containing the document.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>If you provide a target in the request path, it is used for any actions that don't explicitly specify an <code>_index</code> argument.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          It is not possible to index a single document that exceeds the size limit, so you must pre-process any such documents into smaller pieces before sending them to Elasticsearch.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          For instance, split documents into pages or chapters before indexing them, or store raw binary data in a system outside Elasticsearch and replace the raw data with a link to the external system in the documents that you send to Elasticsearch.</p>

./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>Create a new document in the index.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>You can index a new JSON document with the <code>/&lt;target&gt;/_doc/</code> or <code>/&lt;target&gt;/_create/&lt;_id&gt;</code> APIs
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          Using <code>_create</code> guarantees that the document is indexed only if it does not already exist.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          It returns a 409 response when a document with a same ID already exists in the index.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>If the Elasticsearch security features are enabled, you must have the following index privileges for the target data stream, index, or index alias:</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <li>To add a document using the <code>PUT /&lt;target&gt;/_create/&lt;_id&gt;</code> or <code>POST /&lt;target&gt;/_create/&lt;_id&gt;</code> request formats, you must have the <code>create_doc</code>, <code>create</code>, <code>index</code>, or <code>write</code> index privilege.</li>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <li>To automatically create a data stream or index with this API request, you must have the <code>auto_configure</code>, <code>create_index</code>, or <code>manage</code> index privilege.</li>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>Automatic data stream creation requires a matching index template with data stream enabled.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>If the request's target doesn't exist and matches an index template with a <code>data_stream</code> definition, the index operation automatically creates the data stream.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>If the target doesn't exist and doesn't match a data stream template, the operation automatically creates the index and applies any matching index templates.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>NOTE: Elasticsearch includes several built-in index templates. To avoid naming collisions with these templates, refer to index pattern documentation.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>If no mapping exists, the index operation creates a dynamic mapping.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>Automatic index creation is controlled by the <code>action.auto_create_index</code> setting.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          If it is <code>true</code>, any index can be created automatically.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          You can modify this setting to explicitly allow or block automatic creation of indices that match specified patterns or set it to <code>false</code> to turn off automatic index creation entirely.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>NOTE: The <code>action.auto_create_index</code> setting affects the automatic creation of indices only.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>When setting up explicit mapping, you can also use the <code>_routing</code> field to direct the index operation to extract the routing value from the document itself.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          If the <code>_routing</code> mapping is defined and set to be required, the index operation will fail if no routing value is provided or extracted.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>The index operation is directed to the primary shard based on its route and performed on the actual node containing this shard.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>To improve the resiliency of writes to the system, indexing operations can be configured to wait for a certain number of active shard copies before proceeding with the operation.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          This default can be overridden in the index settings dynamically by setting <code>index.write.wait_for_active_shards</code>.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>Valid values are all or any positive integer up to the total number of configured copies per shard in the index (which is <code>number_of_replicas</code>+1).
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>For example, suppose you have a cluster of three nodes, A, B, and C and you create an index index with the number of replicas set to 3 (resulting in 4 shard copies, one more copy than there are nodes).
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          If you attempt an indexing operation, by default the operation will only ensure the primary copy of each shard is available before proceeding.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          This means that even if B and C went down and A hosted the primary shard copies, the indexing operation would still proceed with only one copy of the data.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          If <code>wait_for_active_shards</code> is set on the request to <code>3</code> (and all three nodes are up), the indexing operation will require 3 active shard copies before proceeding.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          However, if you set <code>wait_for_active_shards</code> to <code>all</code> (or to <code>4</code>, which is the same in this situation), the indexing operation will not proceed as you do not have all 4 copies of each shard active in the index.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:        :param index: The name of the data stream or index to target. If the target doesn't

./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          If there are multiple source data streams or indices, it will choose the number of slices based on the index or backing index with the smallest number of shards.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <li>Each sub-request gets a slightly different snapshot of the source data stream or index though these are all taken at approximately the same time.</li>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <li>Query performance is most efficient when the number of slices is equal to the number of shards in the index or backing index. Setting <code>slices</code> higher than the number of shards generally does not improve efficiency and adds overhead.</li>

./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:        index: str,
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:        op_type: t.Optional[t.Union[str, t.Literal["create", "index"]]] = None,
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:            t.Union[int, t.Union[str, t.Literal["all", "index-setting"]]]
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>Create or update a document in an index.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>Add a JSON document to the specified data stream or index and make it searchable.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          If the target is an index and the document already exists, the request updates the document and increments its version.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>If the Elasticsearch security features are enabled, you must have the following index privileges for the target data stream, index, or index alias:</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <li>To add or overwrite a document using the <code>PUT /&lt;target&gt;/_doc/&lt;_id&gt;</code> request format, you must have the <code>create</code>, <code>index</code>, or <code>write</code> index privilege.</li>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <li>To add a document using the <code>POST /&lt;target&gt;/_doc/</code> request format, you must have the <code>create_doc</code>, <code>create</code>, <code>index</code>, or <code>write</code> index privilege.</li>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <li>To automatically create a data stream or index with this API request, you must have the <code>auto_configure</code>, <code>create_index</code>, or <code>manage</code> index privilege.</li>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>Automatic data stream creation requires a matching index template with data stream enabled.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>NOTE: Replica shards might not all be started when an indexing operation returns successfully.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>If the request's target doesn't exist and matches an index template with a <code>data_stream</code> definition, the index operation automatically creates the data stream.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>If the target doesn't exist and doesn't match a data stream template, the operation automatically creates the index and applies any matching index templates.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>NOTE: Elasticsearch includes several built-in index templates. To avoid naming collisions with these templates, refer to index pattern documentation.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>If no mapping exists, the index operation creates a dynamic mapping.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>Automatic index creation is controlled by the <code>action.auto_create_index</code> setting.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          If it is <code>true</code>, any index can be created automatically.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          You can modify this setting to explicitly allow or block automatic creation of indices that match specified patterns or set it to <code>false</code> to turn off automatic index creation entirely.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>NOTE: The <code>action.auto_create_index</code> setting affects the automatic creation of indices only.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>When setting up explicit mapping, you can also use the <code>_routing</code> field to direct the index operation to extract the routing value from the document itself.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          If the <code>_routing</code> mapping is defined and set to be required, the index operation will fail if no routing value is provided or extracted.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>The index operation is directed to the primary shard based on its route and performed on the actual node containing this shard.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>To improve the resiliency of writes to the system, indexing operations can be configured to wait for a certain number of active shard copies before proceeding with the operation.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          This default can be overridden in the index settings dynamically by setting <code>index.write.wait_for_active_shards</code>.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>Valid values are all or any positive integer up to the total number of configured copies per shard in the index (which is <code>number_of_replicas</code>+1).
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>For example, suppose you have a cluster of three nodes, A, B, and C and you create an index index with the number of replicas set to 3 (resulting in 4 shard copies, one more copy than there are nodes).
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          If you attempt an indexing operation, by default the operation will only ensure the primary copy of each shard is available before proceeding.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          This means that even if B and C went down and A hosted the primary shard copies, the indexing operation would still proceed with only one copy of the data.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          If <code>wait_for_active_shards</code> is set on the request to <code>3</code> (and all three nodes are up), the indexing operation will require 3 active shard copies before proceeding.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          However, if you set <code>wait_for_active_shards</code> to <code>all</code> (or to <code>4</code>, which is the same in this situation), the indexing operation will not proceed as you do not have all 4 copies of each shard active in the index.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>Each indexed document is given a version number.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          <p>When using the external version type, the system checks to see if the version number passed to the index request is greater than the version of the currently stored document.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          If true, the document will be indexed and the new version number used.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/__init__.py:          If the value provided is less than or equal to the stored document's version number, a version conflict will occur and the index operation will fail. For example:</p>

./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:        if index not in SKIP_IN_PATH:
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:            __path_parts = {"index": _quote(index)}
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:            __path = f'/{__path_parts["index"]}/_cache/clear'
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:        index: str,
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:            t.Union[int, t.Union[str, t.Literal["all", "index-setting"]]]
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>Clone an index.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>Clone an existing index into a new index.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          Each original primary shard is cloned into a new primary shard in the new index.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>IMPORTANT: Elasticsearch does not apply index templates to the resulting index.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          The API also does not copy index metadata from the original index.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          Index metadata includes aliases, index lifecycle management phase definitions, and cross-cluster replication (CCR) follower information.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          For example, if you clone a CCR follower index, the resulting clone will not be a follower index.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>The clone API copies most index settings from the source index to the resulting index, with the exception of <code>index.number_of_replicas</code> and <code>index.auto_expand_replicas</code>.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          To set the number of replicas in the resulting index, configure these settings in the clone request.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <li>First, it creates a new target index with the same definition as the source index.</li>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <li>Then it hard-links segments from the source index into the target index. If the file system does not support hard-linking, all segments are copied into the new index, which is a much more time consuming process.</li>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <li>Finally, it recovers the target index as though it were a closed index which had just been re-opened.</li>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <li>The index must be marked as read-only and have a cluster health status of green.</li>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <li>The target index must not exist.</li>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <li>The source index must have the same number of primary shards as the target index.</li>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <li>The node handling the clone process must have sufficient free disk space to accommodate a second copy of the existing index.</li>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>The current write index on a data stream cannot be cloned.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          In order to clone the current write index, the data stream must first be rolled over so that a new write index is created and then the previous write index can be cloned.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>NOTE: Mappings cannot be specified in the <code>_clone</code> request. The mappings of the source index will be used for the target index.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>The <code>_clone</code> API returns as soon as the target index has been added to the cluster state, before any shards have been allocated.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          If, for any reason, the target index can't be allocated, its primary shard will remain unassigned until it can be allocated on that node.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>Because the clone operation creates a new index to clone the shards to, the wait for active shards setting on index creation applies to the clone index action as well.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:        :param index: Name of the source index to clone.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:        :param target: Name of the target index to create.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:        :param aliases: Aliases for the resulting index.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:        :param settings: Configuration options for the target index.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:            up to the total number of shards in the index (`number_of_replicas+1`).
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:        if index in SKIP_IN_PATH:
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:            raise ValueError("Empty value passed for parameter 'index'")
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:            "index": _quote(index),
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:        __path = f'/{__path_parts["index"]}/_clone/{__path_parts["target"]}'
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:        index: t.Union[str, t.Sequence[str]],
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:            t.Union[int, t.Union[str, t.Literal["all", "index-setting"]]]
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>Close an index.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>A closed index is blocked for read or write operations and does not allow all operations that opened indices allow.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          It is not possible to index documents or to search for documents in a closed index.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          Closed indices do not have to maintain internal data structures for indexing or searching documents, which results in a smaller overhead on the cluster.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>When opening or closing an index, the master node is responsible for restarting the index shards to reflect the new state of the index.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          An error is thrown if the request explicitly refers to a missing index.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:        :param index: Comma-separated list or wildcard expression of index names used
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:        :param allow_no_indices: A setting that does two separate checks on the index
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:            after all expressions are evaluated. If `true`, index expressions that resolve
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:        :param expand_wildcards: Type of index that wildcard patterns can match. If the
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:            a concrete (non-wildcarded) index, alias, or data stream that is missing,
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:            up to the total number of shards in the index (`number_of_replicas+1`).
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:        if index in SKIP_IN_PATH:
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:            raise ValueError("Empty value passed for parameter 'index'")

./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:        index: str,
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:            t.Union[int, t.Union[str, t.Literal["all", "index-setting"]]]
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>Create an index.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>You can use the create index API to add a new index to an Elasticsearch cluster.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          When creating an index, you can specify the following:</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <li>Settings for the index.</li>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <li>Mappings for fields in the index.</li>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>By default, index creation will only return a response to the client when the primary copies of each shard have been started, or the request times out.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          The index creation response will indicate what happened.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          For example, <code>acknowledged</code> indicates whether the index was successfully created in the cluster, <code>while shards_acknowledged</code> indicates whether the requisite number of shard copies were started for each shard in the index before timing out.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          Note that it is still possible for either <code>acknowledged</code> or <code>shards_acknowledged</code> to be <code>false</code>, but for the index creation to be successful.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          If <code>acknowledged</code> is false, the request timed out before the cluster state was updated with the newly created index, but it probably will be created sometime soon.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          If <code>shards_acknowledged</code> is false, then the request timed out before the requisite number of shards were started (by default just the primaries), even if the cluster state was successfully updated to reflect the newly created index (that is to say, <code>acknowledged</code> is <code>true</code>).</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>You can change the default of only waiting for the primary shards to start through the index setting <code>index.write.wait_for_active_shards</code>.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:        :param index: Name of the index you wish to create. Index names must meet the
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:        :param aliases: Aliases for the index.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:        :param mappings: Mapping for fields in the index. If specified, this mapping
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:        :param settings: Configuration options for the index.

./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>For example, a TSDS index that contains metrics sampled every 10 seconds can be downsampled to an hourly index.

./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>Open a closed index.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>A closed index is blocked for read/write operations and does not allow all operations that opened indices allow.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          It is not possible to index documents or to search for documents in a closed index.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          This allows closed indices to not have to maintain internal data structures for indexing or searching documents, resulting in a smaller overhead on the cluster.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>When opening or closing an index, the master is responsible for restarting the index shards to reflect the new state of the index.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          An error is thrown if the request explicitly refers to a missing index.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>Because opening or closing an index allocates its shards, the <code>wait_for_active_shards</code> setting on index creation applies to the <code>_open</code> and <code>_close</code> index actions as well.</p>

./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>Create or update an index template.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>Elasticsearch applies templates to new indices based on an wildcard pattern that matches the index name.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          Index templates are applied during data stream or index creation.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          Settings and mappings specified in a create index API request override any settings or mappings specified in an index template.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          Changes to index templates do not affect existing indices, including the existing backing indices of a data stream.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>You can use C-style <code>/* *\\/</code> block comments in index templates.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>If multiple index templates match the name of a new index or data stream, the template with the highest priority is used.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>Multiple templates with overlapping index patterns at the same priority are not allowed and an error will be thrown when attempting to create a template matching an existing index template at identical priorities.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>When multiple component templates are specified in the <code>composed_of</code> field for an index template, they are merged in the order specified, meaning that later component templates override earlier component templates.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          Any mappings, settings, or aliases from the parent index template are merged in next.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          Finally, any configuration on the index request itself is merged.

./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>Reload an index's search analyzers and their resources.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>NOTE: This API does not perform a reload for each shard of an index.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          Instead, it performs a reload for each node containing index shards.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          As a result, the total shard count returned by the API can differ from the number of index shards.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          Because reloading affects every node with an index shard, it is important to update the synonym file on every data node in the cluster--including nodes that don't contain a shard replica--before using this API.

./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>Resolve the specified index expressions to return information about each cluster, including the local &quot;querying&quot; cluster, if included.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          If no index expression is provided, the API will return information about all the remote clusters that are configured on the querying cluster.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>You use the same index expression with this endpoint as you would for cross-cluster search.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>For each cluster in the index expression, information is returned about:</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <li>Whether the querying (&quot;local&quot;) cluster is currently connected to each remote cluster specified in the index expression. ./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <li>Whether there are any indices, aliases, or data streams on that cluster that match the index expression.</li>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <li>Whether the search is likely to have errors returned when you do the cross-cluster search (including any authorization errors if you do not have permission to query the index).</li>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>For example, <code>GET /_resolve/cluster/my-index-*,cluster*:my-index-*</code> returns information about the local cluster and all remotely configured clusters that start with the alias <code>cluster*</code>.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          Each cluster returns information about whether it has any indices, aliases or data streams that match <code>my-index-*</code>.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>The ability to query without an index expression was added in version 8.18, so when
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          querying remote clusters older than that, the local cluster will send the index
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          to that index expression even though you didn't request it. If it causes a problem, you can
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          instead include an index expression like <code>*:*</code> to bypass the issue.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>You may want to exclude a cluster or index from a search when:</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <li>A cluster has no matching indices, aliases or data streams for the index expression (or your user does not have permissions to search them). For example, suppose your index expression is <code>logs*,remote1:logs*</code> and the remote1 cluster has no indices, aliases or data streams that match <code>logs*</code>. In that case, that cluster will return no results from that cluster if you include it in a cross-cluster search.</li>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <li>The index expression (combined with any query parameters you specify) will likely cause an exception to be thrown when you do the search. ./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>Roll over to a new index.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>TIP: We recommend using the index lifecycle rollover action to automate rollovers. However, Serverless does not support Index Lifecycle Management (ILM), so don't use this approach in the Serverless context.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>The rollover API creates a new index for a data stream or index alias.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>If you roll over a data stream, the API creates a new write index for the stream.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          The stream's previous write index becomes a regular backing index.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p><strong>Roll over an index alias with a write index</strong></p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>TIP: Prior to Elasticsearch 7.9, you'd typically use an index alias with a write index to manage time series data.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>If an index alias points to multiple indices, one of the indices must be a write index.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          The rollover API creates a new write index for the alias with <code>is_write_index</code> set to <code>true</code>.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          The API also <code>sets is_write_index</code> to <code>false</code> for the previous write index.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p><strong>Roll over an index alias with one index</strong></p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>If you roll over an index alias that points to only one index, the API creates a new index for the alias and removes the original index from the alias.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>NOTE: A rollover creates a new index and is subject to the <code>wait_for_active_shards</code> setting.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p><strong>Increment index names for an alias</strong></p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>When you roll over an index alias, you can specify a name for the new index.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          If you don't specify a name and the current index ends with <code>-</code> and a number, such as <code>my-index-000001</code> or <code>my-index-3</code>, the new index name increments that number.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          For example, if you roll over an alias with a current index of <code>my-index-000001</code>, the rollover creates a new index named <code>my-index-000002</code>.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          This number is always six characters and zero-padded, regardless of the previous index's name.</p>
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          <p>If you use an index alias for time series data, you can use date math in the index name to track the rollover date.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          For example, you can create an alias that points to an index named <code>&lt;my-index-{now/d}-000001&gt;</code>.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          If you create the index on May 6, 2099, the index's name is <code>my-index-2099.05.06-000001</code>.
./venv/lib/python3.14/site-packages/elasticsearch/_sync/client/indices.py:          If you roll over the alias on May 7, 2099, the new index's name is <code>my-index-2099.05.07-000002</code>.</p>
